findings.title: POST /plugins/timeblocking/rpc is an open RPC dispatcher with no schema validation
findings.detail: The timeblocking RPC endpoint accepts `{ method: string, args: unknown[] }`, uses a switch on `method`, and casts `args` elements with `as string`, `as string[]` etc. None of the args are validated. If the method names were to grow or the store API changed, there would be no compile-time or runtime safety on the RPC boundary. This is a 73-line switch block that acts as a hand-rolled RPC dispatcher. A proper validation layer per method or typed Zod discriminated union would make this safer.
findings.impact: Medium — this is the largest unvalidated mutation surface in the API layer. Currently only used by the timeblocking plugin UI.

findings.title: React contexts all follow correct memoization patterns
findings.detail: All 6 context providers (Settings, Task, Plugin, AI, Voice, Undo) use useMemo for their context value objects and useCallback for stable function references. No inline object/array literals in JSX value props were found. useEffect dependency arrays are correct: TaskContext's refreshTasks in [] deps is fine (useCallback with no deps), AIContext's refreshConfig in [] is fine (useCallback with no deps), PluginContext correctly includes all refresh callbacks, SettingsContext's mount-only effect with [] is intentional. No issues found.
